Advisory audit finding: Sense check SC4 is missing an executor acknowledgment.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added the five requested peer-clear tests in `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx` at lines 167-234, using a provider-backed consumer that optionally registers a spy via `registerPeerClear` (lines 17-37). Ran the full test file with `npx vitest run src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx`; it passed with 8/8 tests, including the 'no peer clear registered' case confirming non-toggle selection does not throw when the peer ref remains null.

Wired `useTimelineState` to consume `clearGallerySelection` and `registerPeerClear` from `useGallerySelection` (line 66). Verified plain timeline selection paths clear gallery first in `selectClip` (lines 68-88), `selectClips` (lines 90-100), `clearSelection` (lines 114-118), and the `selectedClipId` sync layout effect (lines 155-162), with `clearGallerySelection` added to each dependency array. Added `clearTimelineOnly` plus peer registration effect at lines 120-130; this callback only calls `clearSelectionState()` and `dataHook.setSelectedClipId(null)`, so gallery-originated peer clears do not recurse back into gallery clearing. Full video-editor regression execution is deferred to T4.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The five peer-clear cases were added at `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx:167-234`, and the full test file passed via `npx vitest run src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx` with 8/8 tests green. The 'allows non-toggle selection when no peer clear is registered' case confirms no error when `peerClearRef` is still null.

Yes. Timeline-originated plain selection paths call `clearGallerySelection()` directly in `useTimelineState` (`selectClip`, `selectClips`, `clearSelection`, and the `selectedClipId` sync effect), and `clearGallerySelection` remains a direct gallery state clear rather than a selection action. Gallery-originated peer clear now resolves to `clearTimelineOnly()` in `useTimelineState:120-130`, which only runs `clearSelectionState()` and `dataHook.setSelectedClipId(null)` and never calls `clearGallerySelection()`. Both directions terminate without a loop.
